species: Candidatus Nanopelagicales (freshwater Actinobacteriota, genome-streamlined, acI lineage)

growth_records.strain: MAG-based population (n=41 MAGs), not axenic culture
growth_records.medium: Freshwater reservoir water column (epilimnion), Římov Reservoir, Czech Republic
growth_records.cite: Kavagutti et al. High-resolution metagenomic reconstruction of the freshwater spring bloom. Microbiome (2023). DOI:10.1186/s40168-022-01451-4. [PMC9878933]
growth_records.snippet: Ca. Nanopelagicales (41) 72.12 ± 16.68 [CARD-FISH, Observed in situ, h]... growth rate r = 1/(t1-t0)*ln(N2/N1)... doubling time may be computed as ln(2)/r
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (72.12 ± 16.68 h) is the paper's own CARD-FISH-based 'Observed, in situ' value from Table 1 (mortality-uncorrected average across the time series). growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.693/72.12 = 0.0096 h-1. Paper also reports an alternative metagenomic-abundance-based in situ estimate (44.44 ± 7.69 h), mortality-corrected estimates (36.67 h CARD-FISH-based; 27.86 h metagenomic-based), and a genome-codon-bias (gRodon) estimate (10.67 ± 7.15 h) for the same taxon group -- these are not separately recorded here. Not an axenic culture; organism is an uncultured candidate taxon reconstructed from MAGs; medium/temperature/pH of the water column were not held constant (varied over the 37-day spring bloom time series). [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature]
